African Sports Technology Network

What is sports technology?

Sports technology is the application of technology to enhance, analyse, commercialise, and grow sport - from elite performance analysis to fan engagement platforms to medical diagnostics for athletes.

A reference for sport organisations, technology companies, investors, and founders exploring the African sports tech landscape.

See how the sector is structured African ecosystem →
The sector

Sports technology is broad - and growing

The term covers any technology applied to sport - whether that improves how athletes train and recover, how fans engage with their sport, how organisations manage their operations, or how the business of sport generates revenue. It spans software, hardware, data, materials science, and health technology.

Performance technology

Tools that enhance athlete training, recovery, and performance - wearables, biomechanics analysis, nutrition tracking, and injury prevention systems.

Business of sport

Technology supporting the commercial side of sport - fan engagement platforms, media and broadcasting tools, ticketing, e-sports, fantasy, and venue operations.

Infrastructure technology

The enabling layer - connectivity, data infrastructure, cloud platforms, and the hardware and sensors that underpin the rest of the sector.

Market structure

How the sports tech market is organised

Sports technology applications can be grouped into three market layers. Understanding this helps organisations identify where technology applies to their work - and helps technology companies position their products accurately.

Professional & Elite Sport — the context for all verticals below

All applications operate within or in service of the professional and elite sport environment

Business of sport & entertainment
Stadium & venue operations
Media & broadcasting
Social media & fan engagement
Prediction markets
eSports & fantasy
Metaverse
Mass participation & active living
Wearables & sensors
Fitness & wellbeing
Medical & nutrition
Coaching & training
Sports analytics
Apparel & equipment
Athlete performance & injury prevention
Biomechanics Cognition & psychology Athlete recovery Strength & conditioning mHealth Medical diagnostics
AfricanSTN · africanstn.com · Adapted from global sports tech sector frameworks
Technology domains

The four domains that underpin sports tech

Sports technology is enabled by four underlying technology domains. Understanding this helps buyers and vendors identify where products sit, and helps investors map the sector accurately.

Sports technology domains · AfricanSTN
Information & communications technologies
Artificial intelligencePerformance data scienceAV / VR / XRBig data analyticsBlockchainCloud infrastructureDigital twinsVideo & game developmentMedia & broadcastMobile & immersivePrivacy & cyber securitySocial mediaStreaming OTTNatural language processingRemote 5G & broadbandFantasy
Medical, health & biotech
BiomechanicsCognition & psychologyAthlete recoverymHealthNutritionStrength & conditioningMedical & diagnosticsChronic health
Advanced materials
Fibres & textilesCoatings & elastomersNano materialsCompositesThermoplasticsFoam & gels
Sensors & devices
At-home connected fitnessIoT & sensor technologyWearablesBio sensorsVideoRobotics
AfricanSTN · africanstn.com
Sports technology in Africa

A market with significant momentum

Sports technology activity in Africa is concentrated primarily in South Africa, with growing ecosystems developing across the continent. AfricanSTN focuses on South Africa as its operational base, with a continental perspective as the platform grows.

South Africa

The most mature sports tech market on the continent. Strong commercial sport infrastructure across football, rugby, and cricket, a developed regulatory environment including POPIA, and an active technology startup ecosystem make South Africa the natural starting point for any company entering African sport.

Continental scope

AfricanSTN takes a continental view of the sports tech landscape - tracking activity, companies, and developments across all 54 African countries as the platform develops.

Regulatory infrastructure

South Africa's POPIA framework is among the most advanced data protection regimes on the continent, creating both compliance obligations and market confidence for international operators.

AfricanSTN's role

AfricanSTN covers the full sports tech landscape

AfricanSTN operates across the full sports tech sector - performance technology, the business of sport, and the enabling infrastructure layer. The African market requires a broad lens, and the platform reflects that.

Get involved Services

Market access

Connecting companies from all four technology domains with African market opportunities.

POPIA compliance

Registered representation for foreign companies handling South African data.

Market intelligence

Research and directory covering the full ecosystem - companies, funding, and sector developments across Africa.

African Sports Technology Network

Explore what is happening in Africa

AfricanSTN maps the African sports technology ecosystem - companies, markets, funding, and sector intelligence. If your organisation is part of the space, register your interest.
